steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two approaches to dyeing blue jeans. The key contrast in the notes: the traditional method requires mixing hazardous chemicals with water to dissolve indigo, while Desai’s process dyes without chemicals by using nanocellulose, a natural plant-based substance. The correct answer must directly compare the two methods and highlight this contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Though created using a different process, Desai's dye contains the same ingredient as the dye produced by blue jean manufacturers.: Describes a similarity (both use indigo powder), not a difference — the opposite of what the goal requires. ✗
  • Choice B — Nanocellulose is a natural, plant-based substance that separates the molecules of indigo powder, which doesn't dissolve in water.: Describes only nanocellulose; does not compare the two dyeing approaches against each other. ✗
  • Choice C — The traditional approach to dyeing blue jeans is to dip them in a solution containing hazardous chemicals.: Describes only the traditional method; does not contrast it with Desai’s approach. ✗
  • Choice D — Desai's approach substitutes a natural plant-based substance for the hazardous chemicals that manufacturers have traditionally used.: Directly contrasts the two methods — natural plant-based substance (Desai) vs. hazardous chemicals (traditional) — in a single sentence that emphasizes the difference. ✓
That matches Choice D — Desai's approach substitutes a natural plant-based substance for the hazardous chemicals that manufacturers have traditionally used..
